Example: Grid World 
▪
A maze-like problem 
▪
The agent lives in a grid 
▪
Walls block the agent’s path 
 
▪
Noisy movement: actions do not always go as planned 
▪
80% of the time, the action North takes the agent North 
▪
10% of the time, North takes the agent West; 10% East 
▪
If there is a wall in the direction the agent would have 
been taken, the agent stays put 
 
▪
The agent receives rewards each time step 
▪
Small “living” reward each step (can be negative) 
▪
Big rewards come at the end (good or bad) 
 
▪
Goal: maximize sum of (discounted) rewards

Recap: MDPs 
▪Markov decision processes: 
▪States S 
▪Actions A 
▪Transitions P(s’|s,a) (or T(s,a,s’)) 
▪Rewards R(s,a,s’) (and discount γ) 
▪Start state s0 
 
 
▪Quantities: 
▪Policy = map of states to actions 
▪Utility = sum of discounted rewards

Recap: MDPs 
▪Markov decision processes: 
▪States S 
▪Actions A 
▪Transitions P(s’|s,a) (or T(s,a,s’)) 
▪Rewards R(s,a,s’) (and discount γ) 
▪Start state s0 
 
 
▪Quantities: 
▪Policy = map of states to actions 
▪Utility = sum of discounted rewards

Optimal Quantities 
 
▪The value (utility) of a state s: 
V*(s) = expected utility starting in s and acting 
optimally 
 
▪The value (utility) of a q-state (s,a): 
Q*(s,a) = expected utility starting out having 
taken action a from state s and (thereafter) 
acting optimally 
 
▪The optimal policy: 
π*(s) = optimal action from state s

The Bellman Equations 
▪Definition of “optimal utility” via expect imax recurrence gives a 
simple one-step look ahead relationship amongst optimal utility 
values 
 
 
 
 
 
 
▪These are the Bellman equations, and they characterize optimal 
values in a way we’ll use over and over

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is a Markov Decision Process (MDP) in Reinforcement Learning? 
 
a) It is a mathematical framework for solving problems with a set of states, actions, and 
rewards. 
b) It is a neural network used for decision-making in RL. 
c) It is an algorithm used to train agents in RL. 
d) It is a type of optimization function used in RL. 
 
 
Answer: A

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is a Markov Decision Process (MDP) in Reinforcement Learning? 
 
a) It is a mathematical framework for solving problems with a set of states, actions, and 
rewards. 
b) It is a neural network used for decision-making in RL. 
c) It is an algorithm used to train agents in RL. 
d) It is a type of optimization function used in RL. 
 
 
Answer: A

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is a Markov Decision Process (MDP) in Reinforcement Learning? 
 
a) It is a mathematical framework for solving problems with a set of states, actions, and 
rewards. 
b) It is a neural network used for decision-making in RL. 
c) It is an algorithm used to train agents in RL. 
d) It is a type of optimization function used in RL. 
 
 
Answer: A

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What are the components of an MDP? 
 
a) States, actions, observations. 
b) States, actions, rewards. 
c) Observations, actions, rewards. 
d) States, observations, rewards. 
 
 
Answer: B

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What are the components of an MDP? 
 
a) States, actions, observations. 
b) States, actions, rewards. 
c) Observations, actions, rewards. 
d) States, observations, rewards. 
 
 
Answer: B

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What are the components of an MDP? 
 
a) States, actions, observations. 
b) States, actions, rewards. 
c) Observations, actions, rewards. 
d) States, observations, rewards. 
 
 
Answer: B

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the purpose of a transition function in an MDP? 
 
A) It maps states and actions to rewards. 
B) It determines the probabilities of moving from one state to another after taking a 
particular action. 
C) It determines the optimal policy for an agent in an environment. 
D) It calculates the expected value of future rewards. 
 
 
Answer: B

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the purpose of a transition function in an MDP? 
 
A) It maps states and actions to rewards. 
B) It determines the probabilities of moving from one state to another after taking a 
particular action. 
C) It determines the optimal policy for an agent in an environment. 
D) It calculates the expected value of future rewards. 
 
 
Answer: B

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the purpose of a transition function in an MDP? 
 
A) It maps states and actions to rewards. 
B) It determines the probabilities of moving from one state to another after taking a 
particular action. 
C) It determines the optimal policy for an agent in an environment. 
D) It calculates the expected value of future rewards. 
 
 
Answer: B

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the goal of an RL agent in an MDP? 
 
A) To maximize the immediate rewards received in each state. 
B) To minimize the immediate rewards received in each state. 
C) To maximize the discounted sum of future rewards. 
D) To minimize the discounted sum of future rewards. 
 
 
Answer: C

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the goal of an RL agent in an MDP? 
 
A) To maximize the immediate rewards received in each state. 
B) To minimize the immediate rewards received in each state. 
C) To maximize the discounted sum of future rewards. 
D) To minimize the discounted sum of future rewards. 
 
 
Answer: C

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the goal of an RL agent in an MDP? 
 
A) To maximize the immediate rewards received in each state. 
B) To minimize the immediate rewards received in each state. 
C) To maximize the discounted sum of future rewards. 
D) To minimize the discounted sum of future rewards. 
 
 
Answer: C

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the role of a policy in an MDP? 
A) It defines the mapping between states and actions. 
B) It determines the optimal value function for an agent. 
C) It calculates the expected value of future rewards. 
D) It maps states and actions to rewards. 
 
 
Answer: A

Markov Decision Process (MDP) 
• What is the role of a policy in an MDP? 
A) It defines the mapping between states and actions. 
B) It determines the optimal value function for an agent. 
C) It calculates the expected value of future rewards. 
D) It maps states and actions to rewards. 
 
 
Answer: A
